draft.introduction: Arjuna asks which path is superior: devotion to the personal God or contemplation of the unmanifest Absolute. Krishna answers that devotion (bhakti) to the personal form is easier and more accessible.

translation.text: Arjuna said: Those devotees who worship you with ever-steadfast devotion, and those who worship the imperishable unmanifest—which of these are better versed in yoga? The Blessed Lord said: Those who fix their minds on me with supreme faith—them I consider the most devoted. But those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifest—they also attain me.
translation.original_text: arjuna uvāca | evaṃ satata-yuktā ye bhaktās tvāṃ paryupāsate

translator_notes.note: Krishna’s admission that the unmanifest path is “more difficult for embodied beings” is a rare concession to human limitation. It privileges bhakti not as philosophically superior but as practically accessible—a move that shaped the entire bhakti movement.
